description: Frozen CertOps route namespaces and stable routes. Core workspace, machine-token executor, controller observation, cert-manager provisioning intent, job read, job manual-create, evidence, token-management, and workspace kill-switch routes are runtime-stable where implemented and documented in OpenAPI. This contract freezes the namespace shape and auth model so cloud and enterprise overlays, the executor, and the agent can build in parallel without route churn. Provisioning adds only a narrow cert-manager provisioning intent and cluster-bound controller command surface; it does not implement the agent protocol.

notes: Human/dashboard surface. Inherits global session + workspace membership + frozen-workspace guards.
Subject to CSRF protection like other workspace mutations.
The workspace kill-switch settings GET and PUT routes require a human cookie/session user; internal worker bearer credentials are not accepted.
Machine surface for M2 executor writes plus narrow M3 controller observation and provisioning transport. Scoped API-token bearer auth only; machine routes never use cookies.

Allowed scopes are certops:read, certops:events:write, certops:jobs:read, certops:evidence:write, certops:observations:write, and certops:provision:execute. certops:read implies certops:jobs:read only, never a write scope; neither controller scope is implied by another scope and either requires an immutable controllerClusterId binding. Empty required-scope configuration is invalid.

CSRF-exempt only for the exact planned POST machine routes (aggregate executor events, controller observations, provisioning commands, and the two per-job aliases), including Express-compatible case and one trailing slash. Nearby paths remain CSRF-protected.
Ingest payloads are scanned and rejected (422) if they contain private key material.

POST /api/v1/certops/executor/events is the aggregate M2 ingestion route. POST /api/v1/certops/jobs/{jobId}/events and POST /api/v1/certops/jobs/{jobId}/evidence are stable path-scoped M2 machine routes.

The three M2 executor routes retain the same idempotency, redaction, private-key rejection, dedicated pre-parser boundary, rate-limit, and audit behavior. POST /api/v1/certops/executor/observations is passive controller-specific M3 transport. POST /api/v1/certops/executor/provisioning-commands/next is a cluster-bound provision-command transport that redelivers only server-owned controller_provisioning jobs safely without M4 claims, agents, heartbeats, signatures, nonces, or polling protocol. It rejects private material before scope or extension gates and is denied while a workspace is paused.
Outbound-only agent polling surface (M4). The control plane never connects to agents.
Enrollment and steady state use different credentials, by design.
POST /agent/register uses agentBootstrapTokenAuth: a single-use, hashed, expiring bootstrap token an admin creates. Registration returns a per-agent credential, shown once.
/agent/heartbeat, /agent/jobs/claim, /agent/jobs/results use agentCredentialAuth: the issued per-agent credential (bearer). Bootstrap tokens do not authorize steady-state calls.
All routes CSRF-exempt (token-authenticated, non-cookie). Frozen in Phase 0 to reserve the namespace; bodies specified in M4.

privateKeyMaterial.notes: Any request body containing private key material is rejected at the boundary. The control plane never stores, generates, exports, transmits, or processes private key material.

workspaceCertOpsPaused.notes: The M3-A1 workspace-local kill switch blocks new CertOps jobs and future dispatch/provision side effects only. It does not block inventory, status, audit, evidence, or executor event reporting for existing work. It composes with, and never replaces, the deployment-wide certops.enabled rollout flag.
